name: Sobrarbe Trail (GR 19)
name_original: Plan & Gistaín — vuelta por el Valle de Chistau/Gistau
description: This difficult 16.4-mile touring cycling route in the Chistau Valley offers stunning Pyrenean views and passes through charming villages.
region_intro: Hiking around Plan, located in the Huesca province of Spain, offers access to a diverse mountain landscape. The region is characterized by its high-altitude lakes, such as Ibón de Plan, and varied terrain that includes both forested paths and more exposed mountain trails. Elevation gains on routes can be substantial, providing panoramic views of the surrounding Pyrenean environment. Best hiking trails around Plan The most popular hiking route is Ibón de Plan Trail, a 2.1 miles (3.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 17 minutes to complete, leading to the scenic mountain lake. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ibón de Plan – Ibón of Plan (Basa de la Mora) loop from Mendaña, a difficult 8.6 miles (13.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, traversing mountain passes and offering expansive views of the high Pyrenees. Local hikers also love the Lavasar Refuge – Ibón of Plan (Basa de la Mora) loop from El Pucé, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail leading through mountain refuges and towards the Ibón of Plan, often completed in about 2 hours 58 minutes. Hiking around Plan is defined by mountain lakes, high-altitude refuges, and varied forest and alpine terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Plan are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 440 reviews. More than 3,600 hikers have used komoot to explore Plan's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Plan?
A: There are over 25 hiking routes around Plan, offering a diverse range of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these trails over 3,600 times.

Q: What are the different difficulty levels for hiking trails in Plan?
A: Plan offers a variety of trails, including 4 easy routes, 12 moderate routes, and 12 difficult routes. This ensures options for both casual walkers and experienced mountaineers.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see while hiking around Plan?
A: The region is known for its stunning high-altitude lakes, such as the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/795895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibón of Plan (Basa de la Mora)&lt;/a&gt;. You can also encounter mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2385971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de Sahún&lt;/a&gt; and various mountain refuges, including &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4971893" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viadós Refuge&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/877756" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ángel Orús Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, which are often found along the routes.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options suitable for children in Plan?
A: Yes, Plan offers 4 easy hiking routes that are generally suitable for families with children. These trails provide a gentler introduction to the Pyrenean landscape without significant elevation gains.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Plan?
A: Yes, many trails in Plan are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10528066" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibón de Plan – Ibón of Plan (Basa de la Mora) loop from Mendaña&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/28741196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lavasar Refuge – Ibón of Plan (Basa de la Mora) loop from El Pucé&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options that allow you to return to your starting point.

Q: What is the typical duration of hikes in Plan?
A: Hike durations vary significantly. Shorter trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1387354235/ibon-de-plan-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibón de Plan Trail&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in just over an hour, while more challenging loops such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10528066" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibón de Plan – Ibón of Plan (Basa de la Mora) loop from Mendaña&lt;/a&gt; can take several hours, often around 6-7 hours, due to their length and elevation gain.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Plan?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 440 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning mountain lakes, the varied terrain ranging from forests to alpine paths, and the panoramic views of the Pyrenees.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through villages or offer cultural insights?
A: Yes, some routes connect local settlements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15013721" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan de Plan – Village of Chistau loop from San Juan de Plan&lt;/a&gt; offers a chance to explore the local villages and their surroundings, providing a glimpse into the regional culture.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to mountain refuges?
A: Absolutely. Several routes incorporate mountain refuges. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/28741196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lavasar Refuge – Ibón of Plan (Basa de la Mora) loop from El Pucé&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice that passes by a refuge, offering a potential stop or overnight stay for longer treks.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails around Plan?
A: The terrain around Plan is quite varied, ranging from forested paths in lower elevations to more exposed, rocky mountain trails at higher altitudes. You'll encounter significant elevation changes, especially on routes leading to high-altitude lakes and passes, characteristic of the Pyrenean environment.

Q: Are there any trails with significant elevation gain for a challenging hike?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10528066" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ibón de Plan – Ibón of Plan (Basa de la Mora) loop from Mendaña&lt;/a&gt; feature substantial elevation gain, exceeding 900 meters. These difficult trails traverse mountain passes and offer expansive views, rewarding experienced hikers.
tips: Plan is the first town you find when you enter the Gistau valley, and which gives its name to several toponyms in the studied valley. This town is located on the north-west side of the valley, which faces the Mediodía mountain. Near it, there is the famous Ibon de Plan, close to two thousand meters.

Chistén is an Aragonese municipality in the Sobrarb region and can be reached from the road that leaves from Sant Juan de Plan. Thanks to its high altitude we can find incredible views of the Valle de Gistaín.

Beautiful valley that is surrounded by peaks between 2,000 and 3,000 meters high, among which the Tuca Llardana (or Posets) stands out, which with 3,375 m. it is the second highest peak in the Pyrenees; In addition, it has abundant lakes, such as those of Millares, Barbarisa and Basa de la Mora (or Plan lake).

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, the Plan & Gistaín cycling tour is classified as difficult and requires very good fitness. With approximately 940 meters of elevation gain over 26.4 kilometers, it's a challenging ride. While much of the surface is paved, there are unpaved segments where you might need to push your bike. It's best suited for experienced touring cyclists in solid physical condition.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Plan & Gistaín cycling tour?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and demanding. You'll encounter significant elevation changes, with a total gain of about 940 meters. Much of the route is paved, but be prepared for unpaved sections that can be difficult to ride, potentially requiring you to dismount and push your bike. It's a true high-mountain cycling experience in the Aragonese Pyrenees.

faqs.question: How much time should I allocate for this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: Komoot estimates the cycling duration to be around 2 hours and 32 minutes. However, given the challenging nature of the route, the significant elevation gain, and the stunning scenery, you should plan for a longer experience. Allow extra time for stops in villages, enjoying the views, and navigating any difficult sections, especially if you're not rushing.

faqs.question: Where can I park when cycling the Chistau Valley route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in the villages of Plan and Gistaín. You can typically find parking in or near these villages. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for designated parking areas, especially during peak seasons, to ensure you're parking legally and respectfully.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle in the Sobrarbe-Pirineos Geopark?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on established routes within the Sobrarbe-Pirineos Geopark. However, it's always good practice to respect local regulations, stay on marked trails, and be mindful of the natural environment. If you plan to venture off-route or into specific protected areas, always check for any special requirements.

faqs.question: Is the Plan & Gistaín cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally welcome in the Aragonese Pyrenees, specific regulations can vary. On this challenging touring bicycle route, consider your dog's fitness and ability to keep up with the demanding terrain and distance. Always keep your dog on a leash, especially in villages and near livestock, and ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your pet. Check local guidelines for any specific restrictions in the Chistau Valley.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and landmarks can I expect along the Chistau Valley cycling tour?
faqs.answer: This route offers breathtaking natural beauty. You'll be cycling through the stunning Chistau Valley, surrounded by peaks ranging from 2,000 to 3,000 meters, including views of Tuca Llardana (Posets), the second-highest peak in the Pyrenees. Expect to see numerous glacial lakes, known as "ibons," such as Ibón de Plan (Basa de la Mora). The route also passes through charming traditional villages like Plan and Gistaín, with their characteristic stone houses and steep alleys.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Chistau Valley route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this high-mountain route is typically during the warmer months, from late spring to early autumn (roughly June to September). During these months, the weather is generally more stable, and snow has usually melted from the higher passes. Always check the local weather forecast before you go, as mountain conditions can change rapidly.

faqs.question: Does this cycling route connect with other trails in the Chistau Valley?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other trails. You'll find sections that overlap with routes like Senderos del Sobrarbe, Plan - Gistain - San Juan de Plan - Plan - Serveto, and Sobrarbe. BTT. Ruta 4. Por el Valle de Chistau. This offers opportunities to extend or modify your ride if you're looking for different challenges or to explore more of the region.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the cycling route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through traditional villages like Plan and Gistaín. These villages typically offer options for accommodation, as well as cafes, restaurants, or small shops where you can refuel and find refreshments. It's a great opportunity to experience the local culture and cuisine.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a touring bicycle trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the challenging nature and mountain environment, pack essentials like a helmet, appropriate cycling attire (including layers for changing weather), plenty of water and snacks, a repair kit for your bike, a first-aid kit, and sun protection. A reliable navigation device is also highly recommended. Don't forget your camera for the stunning views!
